title: vaccinium gitingense
intro: Vaccinium gitingense is a species of flowering plant in the family Ericaceae, commonly known as the blueberry or highland blueberry. Native to the Philippines, this plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and other ailments. The leaves and fruits of Vaccinium gitingense are rich in antioxidants and have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, offering numerous health benefits such as reducing oxidative stress and improving cardiovascular health. Vaccinium gitingense is typically prepared as a tea, infusion, or decoction, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties and reap its health benefits.
